seo.title: Marmalade and Cinnamon Pumpkin - Marinda Kook

html:
I can't give quantities for this recipe. What I can say is that it's one of the tastiest pumpkin recipes in pumpkin land.!!!!!!!!

Just follow the stages step by step and season to taste.

  1. peel the pumpkin and cut it into blocks. Place it in an oven dish
  2. sprinkle salt over – not too much
  3. lay a few cinnamon sticks/bark on the pumpkin.
  4. put spoonfuls of marmalade on the pumpkin – as much as your heart tells you.
  5. now sprinkle plenty of cinnamon sugar over
  6. put spoonfuls of butter on top of the cinnamon sugar
  7. bake it with foil for 30 minutes and without foil for another 30 minutes at 180 degrees Celsius

You don't need to add any water. The pumpkin draws its own liquid under the foil.

To make cinnamon sugar: mix 250ml sugar with 40 ml ground cinnamon.

The pumpkin is delicious and has a fresh citrus taste. The sugar, butter and marmalade make a caramel syrup at the bottom of the dish. I doubt there will be any left over, but if there is – make a pumpkin pie the next day. Pumpkin has never tasted so good.
